diagrams.title: Agile Knowledge Flow Cycle
diagrams.description: A cyclical diagram showing how knowledge flows through agile sprints
elements: Sprint Planning: Knowledge input (requirements, technical constraints)
Daily Development: Knowledge application and creation
Sprint Review: Knowledge validation through working software demonstration
Sprint Retrospective: Knowledge reflection and organizational learning capture
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how knowledge is acquired, applied, validated, and refined iteratively in agile methodology, emphasizing continuous learning

diagrams.title: Tacit to Explicit Knowledge Conversion in Agile
diagrams.description: An iceberg model showing knowledge types in software engineering and agile conversion mechanisms
elements: Above water (Explicit): Documentation, Code, Specifications, API docs
Below water (Tacit): Experience, Intuition, Context, Judgment
Conversion mechanisms: Pair Programming, Code Reviews, Discussions, Retrospectives
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the challenge that most critical software engineering knowledge is tacit, and agile practices specifically target converting tacit knowledge to explicit forms

diagrams.title: Bus Factor Comparison: Traditional vs Agile Teams
diagrams.description: A network diagram comparing knowledge distribution patterns
elements: Traditional: Hub-spoke model (knowledge concentrated around senior architect)
Agile: Mesh network (distributed knowledge across all team members)
Nodes represent team members, edges represent knowledge sharing connections
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how agile practices create more robust knowledge networks, reducing dependency on any single individual and improving project resilience
